name: PR-G 98 Sil Canyon - Santa Cristina Trail
name_original: Ribeira Sacra – circular por el mirador de Pé do Home y el Cañón del Sil
description: Hike the moderate 10.4-mile Ribeira Sacra trail to the Pé do Home viewpoint for spectacular views of the Sil Canyon and terraced vineyards.
region_intro: Hiking around Parada De Sil offers exploration of the dramatic Sil Canyon, where the Sil River carves a deep gorge with cliffs reaching up to 500 meters. The region features lush forests, including ancient chestnut groves, and the picturesque Mao River canyon with its wooden boardwalks. Numerous viewpoints provide expansive vistas of the river valleys and terraced vineyards. The terrain ranges from riverside paths to higher-altitude trails within the Serra de Cabeza de Meda. Best hiking trails around Parada De Sil The most popular hiking route is PR-G 98 Sil Canyon - Santa Cristina Trail, a 12.0 miles (19.4 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route offers stunning canyon views and leads to the historic Santa Cristina Monastery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Mao Canyon Route, an easy 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This trail features wooden boardwalks along the Mao River, providing accessible riverside views. Local hikers also love the Monastery of Santa Cristina – Xariñas de Castro Viewpoint loop from Parada de Sil, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through forests and to panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Parada De Sil is defined by deep river canyons, ancient chestnut forests, and numerous viewpoints overlooking the Sil and Mao rivers. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to challenging canyon routes. The routes in Parada De Sil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1100 hikers have used komoot to explore Parada De Sil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Parada De Sil?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Parada De Sil, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes through the dramatic Sil Canyon, along the picturesque Mao River, and through ancient chestnut groves. You'll find options for various fitness levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging canyon routes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Parada De Sil?
A: Yes, Parada De Sil offers several easy and family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976182932/rio-mao-canyon-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Mao Canyon Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2-mile (5.1 km) path featuring wooden boardwalks along the Mao River, providing accessible riverside views. Another great choice is the "Sendero de Castaños y Carriozas," a 2.5 km circular trail of low difficulty that winds through ancient chestnut forests.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in the Parada De Sil area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979333447/pr-g-98-sil-canyon-santa-cristina-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-G 98 Sil Canyon - Santa Cristina Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route, approximately 12.0 miles (19.4 km) long, taking over 6 hours to complete. This trail offers stunning canyon views and leads to the historic Santa Cristina Monastery, with significant elevation changes as it navigates the dramatic gorge.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Parada De Sil?
A: Yes, many routes in Parada De Sil are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa Cristina – Xariñas de Castro Viewpoint loop from Parada de Sil&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.5-mile (12.1 km) trail through forests and to panoramic viewpoints. The PR-G 177 Ruta del Cañón del Río Mao also offers circular options, including a 16 km version or a shorter 11.5 km route.

Q: What kind of natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking in Parada De Sil is defined by its spectacular natural features. You'll encounter the deep Sil Canyon with cliffs up to 500 meters high, the picturesque Mao River canyon with its boardwalks, and lush forests including ancient chestnut groves. The region is famous for its numerous viewpoints (miradores) offering breathtaking vistas, such as the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcones de Madrid Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cividade Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and Pé do Home Viewpoint.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can visit the 10th-century Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4012022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa Cristina&lt;/a&gt;, nestled within a beautiful forest, which can be incorporated into several hiking routes. The Necropolis of San Víctor, with its anthropomorphic tombs carved into stone, also provides an intriguing historical element to some trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Parada De Sil?
A: The best time for hiking in Parada De Sil is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, the landscapes are vibrant with spring blooms or autumn colors, and the temperatures are comfortable for longer walks. Summer can be hot, especially in the canyons, while winter can bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Parada De Sil?
A: The trails in Parada De Sil are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the breathtaking panoramic views of the Sil Canyon, the unique experience of walking the Mao River boardwalks, and the serene beauty of the ancient chestnut forests. The well-marked paths and diverse terrain for all abilities are also commonly highlighted.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the powerful energy of the water in the region creates impressive natural features. The course of the Gavias stream, reaching the edge of the cliffs, forms the notable Pozo da Ovella waterfall, which plunges into the Sil gorge. While not explicitly on every trail, the dramatic river canyons themselves offer many cascades, especially after rainfall.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: While the focus of Parada De Sil is its natural beauty, you can find local establishments in the villages. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes. Some trailheads or nearby villages may have small cafes or restaurants where you can refuel after your hike, offering local Galician cuisine.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Parada De Sil?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976182932/rio-mao-canyon-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Mao Canyon Route&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1.5 to 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa Cristina – Xariñas de Castro Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically range from 3 to 4 hours. More difficult and longer routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979333447/pr-g-98-sil-canyon-santa-cristina-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-G 98 Sil Canyon - Santa Cristina Trail&lt;/a&gt;, can take 6 hours or more to complete.
tips: The Vilouxe natural viewpoint offers one of the best views of the meander of Coto das Boedas, in the Sil Canyon. The 700 meters of altitude that fall vertically into the river leave an impressive panoramic view of one of the most emblematic points of the Ribeira Sacra.
Beautiful viewpoint with excellent views of the Sil canyon, near the junction between the Sil and Miño rivers, in the Ribeira Sacra area.

In the Ribeira Sacra, very close to the O Pé do Home viewpoint, in Cerreda (Santiago), there is a natural viewpoint called Pena do Pobre. This is already within the limits of Vilar de Cerreda (Santa Baia) and from it we will obtain fantastic views of the river Sil, Os Chancís and the Santo Estevo reservoir.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ribeira Sacra – circular via the Pé do Home viewpoint and the Sil Canyon trail?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can start from several points. The Pé do Home viewpoint itself is a common starting point, and there is usually parking available nearby. Specific details about large parking lots or public transport options are not extensively detailed, so arriving early, especially during peak season, is advisable.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the Ribeira Sacra region is generally dog-friendly, specific regulations for this particular trail are not detailed. Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, well-behaved dogs on a leash should be fine. Always ensure your dog is accustomed to longer hikes and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Ribeira Sacra region is beautiful year-round, but spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally considered the best times for hiking. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes offer vibrant colors, from spring blossoms to autumn foliage, especially with the terraced vineyards. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Ribeira Sacra region or this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the Ribeira Sacra region. The natural beauty and viewpoints are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike features a variety of surfaces. You'll encounter dedicated hiking paths, unpaved sections, paved areas, and some street portions. While mostly accessible, some paths leading to viewpoints can be quite steep, so sure-footedness is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for its spectacular views of the Sil Canyon. Key highlights include the iconic Pé do Home viewpoint, offering expansive vistas of the Sil River, the Os Chancís area, and the Santo Estevo reservoir. Other notable spots include the View of the Coto das Boedas Meander from Vilouxe, Mirador das Escadas, and the View of the Sil River from Pena do Pobre.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 71% of this route overlaps with sections of the official Transourensán trail. This means you'll be sharing parts of the path with other hikers exploring the broader Ribeira Sacra network.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: Information about specific restrooms or designated water sources directly on the trail is not available. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire 5-hour hike and use facilities before starting or in nearby villages.

faqs.question: What makes the Ribeira Sacra region unique for hiking?
faqs.answer: The Ribeira Sacra region is unique for its dramatic Sil Canyon, with vertical cliffs up to 500 meters high, and its distinctive 'heroic viticulture' – terraced vineyards clinging to steep slopes. The area also boasts a rich ecosystem, dense forests, granite rock formations, and a strong cultural heritage, including ancient monasteries, offering a blend of natural beauty and history.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, 16.8 km distance, and approximately 5-hour duration, this trail is best suited for families with older children who are experienced hikers and have a good level of fitness. Younger children might find the length and some steep sections challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the Pé do Home viewpoint?
faqs.answer: While the Pé do Home viewpoint is a popular spot, specific cafes or restaurants directly at the viewpoint are not mentioned. However, the Ribeira Sacra region has charming villages nearby where you can find local eateries to enjoy traditional Galician cuisine after your hike.

waypoints.name: View of the Coto das Boedas Meander from Vilouxe
View of the Sil River from Pena do Pobre
